task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: C_PIN is an object table whose CharSet column 0x04 has type byte_table_ref. CharSet references the byte table used for TPer-generated PIN values, and byte_table_ref type checking SHALL validate that the uidref points to a byte table. A C_PIN object UID is not a byte table reference, so a successful Set storing that UID in CharSet is invalid.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.12 Credential Table Group - C_PIN (Object Table) The C_PIN table contains one row for each password credential. The C_PIN object with UID=0x00 0x00 0x00 0x0B 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x01 and Name=“SID” is the default SID object. Table 181 C_PIN Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|-------------|----------|---------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | Name | Yes | name | | 0x02 | CommonName | Yes | name | | 0x03 | PIN | | password | | 0x04 | CharSet | | byte_table_ref | | 0x05 | TryLimit | | uinteger_4 | | 0x06 | Tries | | uinteger_4 | | 0x07 | Persistence | | boolean | ```

5.3.2.12.5 CharSet This is a reference to the byte table that holds the character set used for TPer-generated PIN column values created using the GenKey method. If the value of this column is a NULL UID reference, then the default character set is used with the GenKey method. See 5.3.4.1.1.1 for a description of the use of this column.

5.1.3.13 byte_table_ref This is a reference type that SHALL be used specifically for uidrefs to byte tables. When performing type checking, as part of that type checking the TPer SHALL validate that this uidref is to a table that is a byte table. Table 63 byte_table_ref | UID | Name | Format | Notes | |---------------------------|-----------------|-----------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 00 00 00 05 00 00 10 01 | byte_table_ref | General_Reference_Table_Type | 2 | This is a reference type that SHALL be used specifically for uidrefs to byte tables. When performing type checking, as part of that type checking the TPer SHALL validate that this uidref is to a table that is a byte table.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity
